NAME: GWANN
DESCRIPTION: GWANN (Genome-Wide Association Neural Networks) is a novel approach that uses neural networks to perform gene-level association studies. Applied to Alzheimer's disease in UK Biobank, GWANN identifies genes linked to family history of AD by aggregating SNP-level information at the gene level through neural network architectures. Published in Briefings in Bioinformatics.
MANUAL_CITATION: Ghose U, Sproviero W, Winchester L, et al. (2024) Genome-wide association neural networks identify genes linked to family history of Alzheimer's disease. Brief Bioinform. 26(1):bbae704. doi:10.1093/bib/bbae704. PMID 39775791
TITLE: Genome-wide association neural networks identify genes linked to family history of Alzheimer's disease.
Authors: Ghose U,Sproviero W,Winchester L,Amin N,Zhu T,Newby D,Ulm BS,Papathanasiou A,Shi L,Liu Q,Fernandes M,Adams C,Albukhari A,Almansouri M,Choudhry H,van Duijn C,Nevado-Holgado A
ABSTRACT: Augmenting traditional genome-wide association studies (GWAS) with advanced machine learning algorithms can allow the detection of novel signals in available cohorts. We introduce "genome-wide association neural networks (GWANN)", a novel approach that uses neural networks (NNs) to perform a gene-level association study with family history of Alzheimer's disease (AD) in UK Biobank.
